center.name: 歐胡島
center.summary: 歐胡島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Hawaiian hawk、Oʻahu ʻelepaio、日本树莺等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The io is one of two extant birds of prey that are native to Hawaii, the other being the pueo (Hawaiian short-eared owl) and fossil evidence indicates that it inhabited the island of Hawaii, Molokai, Oahu, Maui and Kauai at one time. Today, it is known to breed only on the Big Island, in stands of native ōhia lehua (Metrosideros polymorpha) trees

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The fossil record shows that the finch once had a greater range in Hawaii, reaching as far as Oahu, and that birds on Laysan represent a relict population

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date=2007 |title=Biogeography of 'Elepaio: Evidence from Inter-Island Song Playbacks |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/20456016 |journal=The Wilson Journal of Ornithology |volume=119 |issue=3 |pages=325–333 |doi=10.1676/06-015.1 |jstor=20456016 |issn=1559-4491}} They are found in high elevations on the slopes of Oahu{{Cite journal |last1=Vanderwerf |first1=Eric A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In winter, the bush-warbler can also be found in southern China and Taiwan. It was introduced to Oahu between 1929 and 1941 and has since spread throughout the main Hawaiian Islands. In summer, it ranges from low hills to high mountains, preferring bamboo thickets and black pine trees
